steps.title: Target the steel hypothesis
steps.content: The hypothesis is that steel is more suitable than concrete for the lower floors of a tall building. Supporting evidence should show the steel-lower-floor building resisting wind or gravity better than the concrete-lower-floor building.

steps.title: Reject weaker findings
steps.content: A says the steel building bent more in wind, which is a disadvantage. C says vibrations lasted longer in the steel building, another disadvantage. D describes the steel base changing size, which suggests deformation rather than suitability. A concrete building ending shorter than the steel building would show more compression in concrete, so the steel structure better resisted the upper floors' weight over the century. The answer is B.
